Antwortvariable.evidence_anchor: Overall, beech soils showed higher bacterial diversity than pine soils at both the comparable AB and BC horizons

Hauptgruppe_RoteListen.evidence_anchor: In contrast to the beech soils, lower SOC inputs in the pine subsoils affected the community composition, leading to lower CO2 fixation rates.

Lebensraum.evidence_anchor: Rates of dark CO2 fixation per gram of MBC were similar across the beech soil profiles, but significantly lower in the pine soils at the B2 and BC horizons, suggesting that while dark CO2 fixation rates are linked to MBC in deciduous forest soils, other factors influence dark CO2 fixation rates in coniferous forest soils.

Trend.evidence_anchor: Rates of dark CO2 fixation per gram of MBC were similar across the beech soil profiles, but significantly lower in the pine soils at the B2 and BC horizons, suggesting that while dark CO2 fixation rates are linked to MBC in deciduous forest soils, other factors influence dark CO2 fixation rates in coniferous forest soils.

Untergruppe_RoteListen.evidence_anchor: Also, the lower fractions of labile DOC in pine soils promotes the prevalence of fermenters within the Chloroflexi phylum.

Antwortvariable.evidence_anchor: This included _Chloroflexi_ whose quantity differed sharply amongst the soil.

Hauptgruppe_RoteListen.evidence_anchor: Lower CO2 fixation rates in soils due to potential CO2 fixation by microbial fermentation. The pine soils featured a lower fraction of potential CO2 fixers.

Lebensraum.evidence_anchor: Rates of dark CO2 fixation per gram of MBC were similar across the beech soil profiles, but significantly lower in the pine soils at the B2 and BC horizons, suggesting that while dark CO2 fixation rates are linked to MBC in deciduous forest soils, other factors influence dark CO2 fixation rates in coniferous forest soils.

Trend.evidence_anchor: The dominant bacterial community declined with depth in the pine soils.

Die Vegetation von 132 Aufnahmen (nur Wirtschaftswald) des _Hordelymo-Fagetum lathyretosum_
aus dem Zeitraum 1955/67 wurde mit 155 Vegetationsaufnahmen (davon 50 Aufnahmen aus nicht
mehr bewirtschafteten Wäldern) dieser Waldgesellschaft aus dem Zeitraum 2000/12 hinsichtlich

Hauptgruppe_RoteListen.evidence_anchor: As the second largest cause of biodiversity loss worldwide, there is an urgent need to study the dynamics of biological invasions and identify factors limiting the distribution of invasive alien species. In the present study we analyze national-scale hunting bag data from Germany to predict the dispersal of raccoons in the largest non-native population of the species.

Lebensraum.evidence_anchor: Although Germany represents the core of the non-native range in Europe, information about the current status of the raccoon and the patterns of range expansion at a national scale is still rare.
